title: [LICM] Handle disjoint or's in hoistAdd
author: SavchenkoValeriy
description: `disjoint or` is identical to addition and there is no reason not to handle it in `hoistAdd`.

tests.test_body:
define void @or_no_disjoint_neg(ptr %x_p) {
entry:
  %x = load i32, ptr %x_p, align 4, !range !0
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %backedge, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %backedge ]
  %arith = or i32 %iv, %x
  %x_check = icmp slt i32 %arith, 100
  br i1 %x_check, label %exit, label %backedge

backedge:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv, 4
  %loop_cond = icmp slt i32 %iv.next, 1000
  br i1 %loop_cond, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %backedge, %loop
  ret void
}

!0 = !{i32 0, i32 -2147483648}

define void @or_disjoint_unsigned(ptr %x_p) {
entry:
  %x = load i32, ptr %x_p, align 4, !range !0
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %backedge, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %backedge ]
  %arith = or disjoint i32 %iv, %x
  %x_check = icmp ult i32 %arith, 100
  br i1 %x_check, label %exit, label %backedge

backedge:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv, 4
  %loop_cond = icmp ult i32 %iv.next, 1000
  br i1 %loop_cond, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %backedge, %loop
  ret void
}

!0 = !{i32 0, i32 2}

define void @or_disjoint_signed(ptr %x_p) {
entry:
  %x = load i32, ptr %x_p, align 4, !range !0
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %backedge, %entry
  %iv = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %backedge ]
  %arith = or disjoint i32 %iv, %x
  %x_check = icmp slt i32 %arith, 100
  br i1 %x_check, label %exit, label %backedge

backedge:                                         ; preds = %loop
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv, 4
  %loop_cond = icmp slt i32 %iv.next, 1000
  br i1 %loop_cond, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %backedge, %loop
  ret void
}

!0 = !{i32 0, i32 -2147483648}
